feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: The code demonstrates strong Python idioms (perfect score) but needs test cases (missing 1.8 points) and refinement of core parsing logic to achieve full scores on correctness and API design. The main gaps are missing test examples and partial implementations in delimiter detection, header handling, and edge case coverage.

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ code_idiomaticness: Perfect score (8/8) - maintains excellent Python idioms and stdlib usage

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: code_testability.test_examples (current: 0%, 1.8 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Includes example test cases? NO
    Expected: Test cases included for full score, or doctest examples for partial score
    Found: No test cases or doctest examples found in content
    → ACTION: Add a comprehensive test section after the main function with at least 5 test cases covering: basic CSV parsing, delimiter auto-detection, header generation, empty file handling, and quoted fields with embedded delimiters. Use pytest format with assert statements.

  FIX 2: code_correctness.edge_case_resilience (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on edge case handling
    Expected: Handle 100% of edge cases without crash
    Found: Handles most edge cases but may miss some scenarios
    → ACTION: Add explicit handling for these additional edge cases: files with only headers (no data rows), files with inconsistent column counts across rows, files with BOM markers, and files with mixed line endings (\r\n, \n, \r). Wrap each case in try-catch blocks with graceful degradation.

  FIX 3: code_correctness.delimiter_detection (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on delimiter detection implementation
    Expected: Full auto-detection from data analysis for 1.0 score
    Found: Good implementation but may have parameter dependency reducing score
    → ACTION: Ensure the detect_delimiter function operates purely on sample data analysis without any parameter dependencies. Remove any fallback to default parameters and make detection entirely data-driven using only the sample content analysis.

  FIX 4: code_correctness.header_handling (current: 75%, 1.1 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on header handling implementation
    Expected: Both auto-detection AND generation capabilities for 1.0 score
    Found: Has both capabilities but implementation may not be fully automatic
    → ACTION: Strengthen the detect_headers function to be more decisive in automatic detection. Add confidence scoring to header detection and ensure the function automatically chooses between using existing headers or generating synthetic ones without requiring user intervention.

  FIX 5: code_api_design.type_hints (current: 75%, 0.7 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on type annotations completeness
    Expected: Complete type annotations on all functions for 1.0 score
    Found: Good type hints but may be missing on some helper functions
    → ACTION: Add complete type annotations to all helper functions including _calculate_delimiter_consistency, _analyze_data_type_consistency, _detect_multiline_headers, and _is_numeric. Ensure every parameter and return value has explicit type hints.

  FIX 6: code_api_design.input_flexibility (current: 75%, 0.7 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on input type support
    Expected: Support for multiple input types for 1.0 score
    Found: Good input flexibility but implementation may not handle all types seamlessly
    → ACTION: Add explicit input type detection and handling logic at the function start. Create a helper function that normalizes all input types (str, Path, file-like, URL) into a consistent string format before processing, with clear error messages for unsupported input types.

  FIX 7: code_api_design.docstring (current: 75%, 0.6 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on docstring completeness
    Expected: Complete docstring with example for 1.0 score
    Found: Has comprehensive docstring but may lack some elements
    → ACTION: Enhance the main docstring by adding a 'Raises:' section documenting specific exceptions (UnicodeDecodeError, MemoryError, etc.) and add docstrings to all helper functions with their specific parameters and return values documented.

  FIX 8: code_testability.pure_function (current: 50%, 0.6 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Partial score on function purity
    Expected: Pure function with no side effects for 1.0 score
    Found: Functions are mostly pure but may have minor side effects
    → ACTION: Remove any logging statements or print calls from the main parsing functions. If logging is needed, make it optional through a parameter and ensure all functions return the same output for the same input without any external state dependencies.
